title: picea jezoensis subsp. jezoensis
intro: Picea jezoensis subsp. jezoensis, also known as the Japanese black spruce, is a coniferous evergreen tree native to the mountainous regions of Japan and the Korean Peninsula. The bark and needles of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, primarily to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Picea jezoensis subsp. jezoensis include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, the bark and needles are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
